title: Eurybia ramulosa
intro: Eurybia ramulosa, also known as Gray's goldenrod or Gray's goldenrod aster, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. The plant has been traditionally used to alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma due to its expectorant properties, which help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs. The bioactive constituents of Eurybia ramulosa include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Eurybia ramulosa is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions to help relieve respiratory congestion and promote overall respiratory health.
taxonomy: Eurybia ramulosa is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Eurybia, which is a group of plants native to North America. The species is further categorized as a subshrub or a small shrub, and is commonly found in rocky outcrops and dry areas. Eurybia ramulosa is also known as the bushy aster, which is a common name for its distinctive appearance. Its classification as a member of the Asteraceae family is due to its characteristics, such as the presence of daisy-like flowers and a composite inflorescence.
